name: Vike
description: Flexible, framework-agnostic SSR/SSG meta-framework (formerly vite-plugin-ssr) with React support

description.value: A flexible, framework-agnostic Vite-based SSR/SSG meta-framework (formerly vite-plugin-ssr) with a React integration via vike-react.
description.comment: Positions itself as a do-one-thing-well alternative to Next.js/Nuxt, built on Vite. Evaluated at v0.4.260 (July 2026).

repository.comment: Official GitHub repository under the vikejs org.

license.comment: SPDX MIT per GitHub license metadata and the npm package manifest.

category.comment: Vike owns the Vite-based build/server toolchain and routing is one part of it; per the guideline a full framework gets the meta-framework tag.

active-maintenance.comment: Repo pushed 2026-07-09 and releases ship very frequently (v0.4.260 on 2026-06-28) — clearly active.

initial-release.comment: Repository created 2021-01-28 as vite-plugin-ssr; first public releases that year. Renamed to Vike in 2023.

latest-release.comment: vike@0.4.260, the latest GitHub release (published 2026-06-28) and current npm `latest`. Vike is pre-1.0 and releases many patch versions.

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Weekly downloads of the primary `vike` package for the week of 2026-07-08 to 2026-07-14.

routing-paradigm.comment: Filesystem routing by default (pages/**/+Page.js); Route Strings/Route Functions declared via +route/+config objects; navigation and pageContext via functions/hooks (navigate(), usePageContext()). Routes are not declared as JSX components.

nested-layout-routes.comment: Shared/nested layouts via the +Layout setting and config inheritance across page groups.

dynamic-params.comment: Dynamic segments like /movie/@id, read via pageContext.routeParams.

optional-catch-all-params.comment: Catch-all/glob segments (e.g. /@*rest) in Route Strings, and full wildcard/regex matching via Route Functions.

search-params-api.comment: pageContext.urlParsed exposes parsed search/searchAll (read-only), but there is no first-class reactive read/write query-state API like useSearchParams; writing query state means navigating to a new URL.

programmatic-navigation.comment: navigate('/some/url') plus reload() (Client Routing); redirect() available in guard()/hooks.

route-guards-middleware.comment: Built-in guard() hook (+guard.js) runs before render to protect pages and can redirect/throw render().

written-in-typescript.comment: Authored in TypeScript with first-class type definitions (vike/types).

type-safe-paths-links.comment: Type-safe links checked against route definitions are still a work-in-progress feature (tracked in issue #698); links are plain <a>/navigate() strings today.

type-safe-params.comment: pageContext.routeParams is typed as a generic string-keyed record, not inferred from the route string; you must narrow/cast it yourself.

type-safe-search-params.comment: No built-in schema-based typing/validation of query/search params.

codegen-required.comment: No route-type codegen step; pageContext typing is done manually by extending the global Vike.PageContext interface via declare global.

type-safety-rating.comment: Fully written in TS with typed pageContext, but paths, params, and search are not checked against routes (params are generic strings, typed links WIP) — matches the guideline's rating 2.

route-data-loaders.comment: Route-level +data() hook runs before render; components read it via useData() (vike-react).

actions-mutations.comment: No built-in route action/mutation primitive; docs recommend external RPC/mutation tools (Telefunc, tRPC, TanStack Query). RSC-style server actions exist only in the experimental vike-react-rsc extension.

prefetching.comment: prefetchStaticAssets ('hover' default, 'viewport' option) and programmatic prefetch() prefetch route code/static assets on intent; note it prefetches static assets, not the page's pageContext/data.

pending-transition-ui.comment: Built-in +onPageTransitionStart / +onPageTransitionEnd hooks let you show a loading/pending indicator during Client Routing navigations.

scroll-restoration.comment: Client Routing restores scroll on back/forward and scrolls to top on navigation by default; +keepScrollPosition / keep-scroll-position preserve position across groups.

per-route-code-splitting.comment: Automatic per-page code splitting via filesystem routing and Vite.

per-route-error-handling.comment: Error handling via the +error-page setting and +onError() hook; error pages can be scoped through config inheritance per page group, though it is closer to a configurable error page than React Router's nested per-route error elements.

ssr-support.comment: Turnkey SSR is a core feature, enabled by default via the vike-react extension with a per-page +ssr toggle.

streaming-ssr.comment: First-class HTML streaming via the +stream setting (react-streaming), with progressive/Suspense rendering and Node + Web Stream support.

react-server-components.comment: RSC is not first-class in core vike-react; it is available only through the separate, experimental (in-progress) vike-react-rsc extension. Standard vike-react users are steered toward RPC (Telefunc) instead of server actions.

static-site-generation.comment: Built-in pre-rendering (SSG) via the +prerender setting emits static HTML at build time; can be mixed with SSR/SPA per page.

client-only-usable.comment: SSR can be disabled globally or per page (+ssr: false) to run as a pure client-side SPA — but still requires the Vike/Vite build toolchain.

framework-bound.comment: Vike requires its own Vite-based build/framework toolchain; it cannot be dropped into an arbitrary React app as a plain library, so per the guideline it is framework-bound.

bundle-size.comment: Vike is a meta-framework, not a standalone router package; a single min+gzip 'router bundle' figure isn't comparable to library routers, so per the guideline this is null.

zero-runtime-dependencies.comment: The vike package ships many runtime/build dependencies (vite, esbuild, @babel/core, cac, magic-string, sirv, etc.).

tree-shakeable.comment: Built on Vite/Rollup with automatic per-page code splitting and dead-code elimination for the app bundle.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive per-setting API reference, guides, migration notes, and tool-integration examples; organization can be dense/fragmented across many +setting pages — matches rating 4.

official-devtools.comment: No dedicated official route/navigation devtools inspector; Vike offers build-time DX warnings and hook timing but no browser inspector panel.

data-fetching-integration.comment: Built-in +data() loader is the primary mechanism; official vike-react-query extension provides first-class TanStack Query integration. tRPC/Telefunc are documented as recommended tools, and RSC integration exists only via the experimental vike-react-rsc extension.

predecessor-lineage.value: Formerly vite-plugin-ssr; renamed to Vike in 2023.
predecessor-lineage.comment: Same project and repository (created 2021 as vite-plugin-ssr), rebranded to Vike.
